memory_server.py snapshot (2026-03-11): 5788 lines. LanceDB tables: knowledge, observations, fix_outcomes, web_pages, quarantine. SQLite: tags.db (TagIndex), knowledge_graph.db (KnowledgeGraph). Active tools (8): search_knowledge, fuzzy_search, remember_this, get_memory, record_attempt, record_outcome, query_fix_history, health_check. Dormant: deduplicate_sweep, delete_memory, cluster_knowledge, get_teammate_context, memory_health_report. RETRIEVAL: hybrid=RRF(k=60) BM25+cosine; tag co-occurrence expansion >40%; graph spreading activation (0.8x discount); Hebbian strengthen_coretrieval top-10. CAUSAL CHAIN: ID={error_hash}_{strategy_hash}; Laplace confidence (s+1)/(n+2); 30d half-life decay; banned if attempts>=2 AND confidence<0.18. KNOWLEDGE GRAPH: Hebbian strength += 0.1*(1-strength); BFS max_hops=3; edge decay 168h half-life, prune <0.05.

Shodh-Memory deep research (2026-03-09): Rust cognitive memory. KNOWLEDGE GRAPH: RocksDB 9 column families; salience=base*(1+0.1*ln(mention_count)); dedup 4-tier (exact/case/Porter2/cosine>=0.85). SPREADING ACTIVATION: BFS, decay=e^(-rate*hop), rate 0.1-0.4 by importance; bidirectional 1.5x boost; max 6 hops; threshold 0.005. HEBBIAN: boost=(0.1+tier_boost)*(1-strength). DECAY: hybrid; t<3d exponential; t>=3d power-law A_cross*(t/3)^-0.5; LTP factors: none=1.0,burst=0.5,weekly=0.3,full=0.1. LTP THRESHOLDS: burst=5/24h; weekly=3/wk for 2+wks; full=10 activations. THREE-TIER: Working(7+/-2,30s)->Session(~100,30m)->LongTerm(unlimited). RETRIEVAL: semantic(0.5)+graph(0.35)+linguistic(0.15); RRF variant BM25(0.35)+vector(0.40)+graph(0.25) k=45.

Claude-Code-Workflow (catlog22/CCW) (2026-02-28): 1349 stars, TypeScript, MIT, v7.0.0. JSON-driven multi-agent cadence-team framework (Gemini/Qwen/Codex/Claude). ARCHITECTURE: 37 skills+22 agents; team v2 event-driven beat model; queue scheduler; React dashboard; CodexLens (FTS5+embeddings). EXTENSION: via .claude/ agents/commands/skills/ dirs, not hooks/gates. MEMORY: SQLite WAL per-project; entities/access logs/heat scores/conversations; CodexLens HNSWLIB+fastembed; no cross-session transfer. HOOKS: 5 lifecycle hooks (SessionStart, SessionEnd, Stop, UserPromptSubmit, PreCompact); UserPromptSubmit detects magic keywords (autopilot/ralph/swarm). TOKEN: session-start 1500 chars, per-prompt 500 chars. VS TORUS: CLI-first; multi-AI; per-project SQLite not LanceDB; event-driven not gate-based.

Sub-agents vs Agent Teams analysis (2026-02-11): Memory MCP changes tradeoff — sub-agents share state via search_knowledge/remember_this, fix_history, HANDOFF.md/LIVE_STATE.json. TOKEN COST: teams use 30-50% more tokens (SendMessage, TaskList polling, team config reads, idle notifications, shutdown handshakes). QUALITY: teams 30-50% better for interdependent work. REVISED DECISION TABLE: 2-5 independent->sub-agents; 2-5 dependent->sub-agents (memory bridges); 5-7 dependent->either (teams preferred); 7+ dependent->agent teams; cross-session->sub-agents+memory. KEY: Memory=long-term pull-based async; Team messages=short-term push-based sync. Inflection point shifted ~4->~6-7 dependent steps (with memory MCP). Teams win: real-time push, 8+ agents, dynamic re-planning, peer help, progress visibility. CHANGE: updated CLAUDE.md with full decision table. ~/CLAUDE.md is symlink to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md.

Agent Zero technical summary (2026-02-28): Flask async+WebSocket+React+Docker. Core loop: prepare_prompt->call_chat_model->process_tools->break_loop_check. Default model: Claude Sonnet 3.5. CONTEXT: History class hierarchical (Bulks->Topics->Messages); 50/30/20% budgets; 4-tier compression (msg summarize, attention window, topic->bulk, bulk delete). MEMORY: FAISS 3-area (MAIN/FRAGMENTS/SOLUTIONS), 1024d cosine, 0.7 threshold, LLM consolidation (~$0.02-0.05/save), project-isolated. MULTI-AGENT: hierarchical A2A. QUALITY: NO gates; Docker-only safety; known issue #1011 infinite loops; 24 lifecycle hooks but no default enforcement. VS CLAUDE CODE: WebUI vs terminal; FAISS vs none; native multi-agent vs MCP; 25+ providers vs Claude-only; 100-200 token overhead vs 50; cross-session learning vs none; zero safety gates vs 24.

AI agent benchmarking research (Feb 2026). BENCHMARKS: AgentBench (8 envs); GAIA (466 questions, 3 levels); SWE-bench (2294 GitHub issues); WebArena (812 tasks); MINT (GPT-4+Python); ToolEmu (36 high-stakes); Webshop (1.18M products); MetaTool (21K+ prompts); Berkeley FCLB (2K QA pairs). CLEAR FRAMEWORK: Cost(CNA+CPS)+Latency+Efficacy+Assurance(PAS)+Reliability(pass^k); 0.2 per dimension. MEMORY METRICS: Recall@k, Precision@k, F1@k, MRR, Contextual Recall/Precision. MULTI-AGENT (MAESTRO): planning score, communication score, inter-agent msg count, convergence time, duplicate work rate. NORMALIZATION: min-max; pass@k=1-(1-p)^k; CNA=(Accuracy*Cost_Baseline)/Cost_Model. BEST PRACTICE: framework-agnostic scorers. Tools: Context-Bench, LoCoBench-Agent, MAESTRO, Braintrust (OTEL), Scenario.

Claude Code Agent Farm (Dicklesworthstone): github.com/Dicklesworthstone/claude_code_agent_farm, 655+ stars, MIT. 1 Python file (132KB), 47 configs, 54 prompts, 37+ best-practice guides. PURPOSE: process manager for 20-50 parallel Claude Code CLI instances via tmux. COORDINATION: JSON registry (active_work_registry.json, agent_locks/, completed_work_log.json, planned_work_queue.json); stale lock=1-2hr; prompt-based LLM manages lock protocol; commit prefix [Agent:{id}]. CONTEXT MGMT: monitors context% via regex; threshold 20% triggers /clear; Ctrl+R broadcast. SAFETY: no gates; file locking; settings backup 200MB/10 rotations; max_errors=3; exponential backoff 10s/5min. MEMORY: none (session state via .claude_agent_farm_state.json+git+JSON). VS TORUS: horizontal scaling vs vertical depth; no hooks/gates/LanceDB; complementary but architecturally orthogonal.

Heartbeats in AI agent systems (2026-03-09). THREE MEANINGS: (1) distributed-systems liveness ping 1-10s; (2) autonomous-agent scheduled turn (OpenClaw/ZeroClaw) every 30m; (3) agentic coordination expansion/contraction phases. OPENCLAW: agents.defaults.heartbeat.every=30m; HEARTBEAT.md checklist per turn; reply HEARTBEAT_OK if nothing to do; lightContext=true saves tokens; bug #14986 per-agent interval ignored. ZEROCLAW: Rust 3.4MB, boots <10ms; config.toml [heartbeat] interval_minutes=30; hot-reload; cron+RFC3339+fixed intervals; ARM/x86/RISC-V. CLAUDE CODE: no native heartbeat; Notification hook on idle (60s, unreliable #8320); pattern: run claude -p via cron every 15min reading REMINDERS.md. TORUS: torus-wave.py polls proc.poll() every 5s; MAX_RETRIES=2; task_timeout+30s grace. DISTRIBUTED: timeout=2-3x interval; never mark dead on first miss.

Multi-Instance Agent Architecture V1 spec: Persistent Claude Code sessions as named role-agents, file-based IPC+tmux, shared memory MCP. DIRS: ~/agents/{role}/ (git repo, CLAUDE.md, LIVE_STATE.json, config.json); ~/projects/{name}/; ~/.claude/channels/ (task_{role}.json, result_{role}.json, status_{role}.json, log.jsonl). CONFIG LAYERS: global CLAUDE.md; role CLAUDE.md; project CLAUDE.md+.agent-config.json (watcher-injected). NAMING: by role not model (researcher-alpha, builder, verifier); memory tagged agent:{role},model:{model}. LAUNCH: cd ~/agents/role && tmux new-session -s {role} 'claude --model {model}'. RESULT STATE MACHINE: working->idle_prompt fires->summarizing->idle. TWO CHANNELS: outbox (ephemeral task answer); shared memory (durable). GATE 13: co-claim concept for concurrent file editing. NOT IN V1: MCP delegate_to(), async delegation, auto-scaling, inter-agent direct comms.

Parallel agent coordination research (Agent Farm + Anthropic C Compiler). SPAWNING: tmux panes (agent_farm); git worktree isolation per branch (ccswarm); Task tool subagents (Claude Code). CONFLICT PREVENTION: JSON registry+lock files (agent_farm); git merge semantics (C compiler); worktree branch exclusivity. TASK DISTRIBUTION: manual random chunk selection; shared task board @mention (agent teams); actor model channels (ccswarm Rust). LIMITATIONS: monolithic tasks fail (all hit same bugs); DB race conditions; API rate limits; 50 agents=50x cost; no cross-agent memory sharing. CEILING: 3-4 practical max; tested 20-50 (agent_farm), 60+ (Claude Flow). NOT SUITABLE FOR TORUS: hooks tied to single-session events; state.json per session_id; Gate 13 blocks concurrent file edits; ChromaDB segfaults under concurrent access. BETTER PATH: built-in subagents or Agent Teams.

Pi agent (badlogic/pi-mono) extensibility research. HOOK SYSTEM: AgentLoopConfig interface -- transformContext(messages,signal), convertToLlm(messages), getSteeringMessages(), getFollowUpMessages(), getApiKey(provider). EVENTS: tool_execution_start/end (reactive post-hoc). TOOL REGISTRATION: setTools(AgentTool[]) -- execute(toolCallId,args,signal,onUpdate?)->AgentToolResult; createCodingTools()/createReadOnlyTools()/createAllTools() presets. EXTENSIBILITY: declaration merging on CustomAgentMessages; no plugin registry. AGENT LOOP: dual-loop (outer: follow-ups; inner: tool calls+steering); agentLoop() vs agentLoopContinue(); convertToLlm called each invocation (no caching). PHILOSOPHY: minimal core, extensions built outside. VS CLAUDE CODE: Pi=event emission post-execution (reactive); Claude Code=PreToolUse gate (imperative, can block); Pi allows steering interrupts between tools.

BMAD-Method research: Breakthrough Method for Agile AI-Driven Development. Multi-layer system (5 layers). YAML agent definitions compiled to markdown with XML activation blocks, installable via npm. 12+ specialized AI personas (PM/Architect/Dev/QA); 34+ workflows (v4), 50+ (v6); memories.md for session-to-session learning. Agent-Zero framework: 15k+ GitHub stars, Python, personal AI assistant. ARCHITECTURE: hierarchical multi-agent (every agent reports to superior); computer-as-tool (writes+executes code); Docker containerized; prompt-driven via prompts/ dir. MEMORY: 3-tier persistent (conversation buffer, facts, solutions); FAISS vector embeddings (cosine); LLM-driven consolidation; project-isolated; memory dashboard v0.9.6+. SAFETY: no published gates; known issue #1011 (repeat loops, no auto-recovery); no loop-breaker or interrupt-on-timeout. TOOLS: web search (SearXNG), code execution, browser automation, terminal, MCP; SKILL.md open standard (YAML+markdown, <5000 tokens, compatible with Claude Code/Cursor). MULTI-AGENT: hierarchical call_subordinate; A2A protocol; parallel delegation supported. LEARNING: persistent memory stores solutions; no causal chain equivalent. VERSION: 0.9.8+ (skills, Git projects, Anthropic caching).

Multi-agent orchestration patterns 2025-2026. CORE: hub-and-spoke (deterministic); mesh (adaptive); supervisor/hierarchical (sub-agents 67% fewer tokens); adaptive network (P2P, emergent). PROTOCOLS: A2A (50+ companies, HTTP/SSE/JSON-RPC 2.0); ACP (REST, multi-vendor); MCP (tools, complementary to A2A). PARALLEL: optimal 3-5 agents; read-only=safe concurrent; state-modifying=sequential. TOKEN ECONOMICS: chat=1x; agent+tools=4x; multi-agent=~15x. FAILURE RECOVERY: Observer Mesh (localized rollback); hierarchical>flat>linear. LOAD BALANCING: RL-based 22% better than static. 2026 TRENDS: human-on-the-loop; market 5.25B to 52.62B 46.3% CAGR; A2A/ACP replacing proprietary. ANTHROPIC: detailed lead instructions; end-state evaluation; durable recovery (checkpoints+retry); rainbow deployments.

Competitive AI framework analysis (5 repos): claude-simone (Helmi, ~518 stars): Python/TypeScript project mgmt with Simone MCP; no gates; moderate token overhead. claude_code_agent_farm (619 stars): Python parallel tmux orchestration for 20+ agents; no mechanical gates; lock-based coordination; low-moderate overhead. hive (adenhq, 7.9k stars): outcome-driven graph execution, 107 tests pass, max_tokens=8192, no built-in gates, SDK-wrapped shared memory, low overhead. get-shit-done (gsd-build, 17.5k stars): TypeScript meta-prompting, 22 tests, thin orchestrator 10-15% context; HIGH overhead (multi-agent 200k token fresh contexts per agent). agent-zero (12k+ stars): Python FAISS vector DB, SKILL.md standard, extension lifecycle hooks, moderate overhead; memory key errors reported. SUMMARY: none have explicit gate systems; stars: gsd>agent-zero>hive>agent_farm>simone.

Arxiv survey: LLM agent memory architecture papers (2025-2026). EPISODIC: arXiv:2502.06975 (when/why/how/who traces for long-term agents); arXiv:2601.21714 E-mem (heterarchical: assistants uncompressed, master orchestrates); arXiv:2511.10652 (affective-semantic metadata: temporal, emotional valence). ADAPTIVE/RL: arXiv:2508.19828 Memory-R1 (ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP Manager + Answer Agent, learns what to store); arXiv:2602.14038 FluxMem (adaptive structures per interaction); arXiv:2601.07470 (meta-cognitive structured transferable memory). MEMORY OS: arXiv:2507.03724 MemOS (lifecycle states+MemScheduler+instant paths, beats all baselines); arXiv:2506.06326 (earlier Memory OS). SURVEYS: arXiv:2512.13564 (token-level/parametric/latent; factual/experiential/working taxonomy); arXiv:2504.15965 (human->AI memory mapping). RAG: arXiv:2505.08445 (Chroma 13% faster, Faiss higher precision); arXiv:2507.03608 (vector vs graph vs hybrid). SEMANTIC: arXiv:2508.12630 (linguistic cues).
